sampleTweets.text:
Solana Ecosystem Map 2026 for Founders {v.2}

The opportunities to build on @solana are massive, so it wasn’t possible to include everything in one post.

Here’s an update: 

1. Investment communities

They can help you get funding on fair terms, often easier than pitching traditional VCs. 

> Monke Ventures {@MonkeVentures}

The investment syndicate of @MonkeDAO, democratizing high-quality deal flow for everyday investors. The syndicate has funded over 40+ teams, with $2M+ in total funding.

> Superteam syndicate at the @echodotxyz

Lets people invest in crypto startups and token projects by joining private funding rounds alongside top investors.

2. Hackatons

Hackathons allow you to test your product in real conditions, receive feedback, secure early funding, and build long-term relationships.

> @colosseum: main {twice a year} and Eternal
> @solanamobile & @RadiantsDAO focused on mobile apps for Seeker
> @superteam's local hackathons

3. Grants {Non-Dilutive Funding}

At an early stage, raising large amounts of venture capital is rare. Better start with smaller, strategic funding that validates your project.
 
> @SolanaFndn {~$40 000}
>  @solanamobile {~$10 000}
> @superteam {~$10 000}
> @Kalshi {$2 000 000 pool}
> @MetaplexFndn {based on DAO decision}

4. Accelerators 

Accelerators provide structured support in product development, go-to-market strategy, fundraising preparation, and network access.

> @colosseum
> @OrangeDAOxyz 
> @incubator
> @alliance
> @MonkeFoundry 
> @a16zcrypto 
> @OnePieceLabs 
> @venture_launch

5. Conferences and IRL Events 

Strategic conversations at events often lead to partnerships, funding, and distribution opportunities.

> Solana Breakpoint & Accelerate by @SolanaEvents
> Events by @MeteoraAG and @JupiterExchange 
> Solana Summits hosted by Superteams

6. Podcasts

To stay in sync with Solana news, opportunities, and learn how others operate, follow pages who consistently share insights.

> Billions hosted by @Pedromiranda

> Open Intelligence hosted by @_rishinsharma

March on Solana was one of those months where you blink and miss three launches. 12 SIMDs activated, Jupiter shipped 8 releases, and a drop-in SPL replacement went live on devnet, cutting transfer compute by 98%. 

If you're subscribed to our newsletter, you already know all of this. You'll find a roundup below, and make sure to subscribe not to miss it next month 👇
https://t.co/K9wSx2aqdG

Anchor 1.0 just shipped, and it's a big one for @solana_devs. Here's what each change means for your workflow:

- No Solana CLI dependency → one fewer tool to install, no more version conflicts

- Surfpool + LiteSVM defaults → Surfpool replaces the local validator for testing, LiteSVM is the new default test template. Faster across the board

- Migration<From, To> → upgrade account schemas on live programs without manual deserialization

- Lifecycle hooks → run linters, security checks, or custom scripts pre/post build, test, and deploy from Anchor.toml

- avm self-update → update AVM without reinstalling through Cargo

- declare_program! parser → decode raw instruction data from the IDL automatically

- Duplicate mutable accounts rejected → prevents a whole class of exploits by default, opt in with dup if you need it

reason: Hack / Exploit cluster linked by [exploit, drift, funds, hack, security]

sampleTweets.text:
🚨 Drift Protocol Hack — Key Update (Apr 3, 2026)
One of the largest DeFi exploits this year has hit Solana.

▫️~$285M drained (est. $270–286M)
▫️ >50% TVL wiped in ~12 minutes
▫️ Assets stolen: USDC, SOL, JLP, WBTC & more
▫️ 2nd largest hack in Solana history (after Wormhole)

 ⚠️ Attack vector (not a code exploit):
The attacker leveraged durable nonces — a legitimate Solana feature — to execute pre-signed admin transactions prepared weeks in advance.
→ Gained control of the Security Council (multisig)
→ Removed withdrawal limits
→ Drained protocol vaults at high speed

👉 Post-exploit flow:
▫️ Funds bridged to Ethereum via Circle’s CCTP
▫️~129K ETH moved cross-chain

🔎 Notable developments:
▫️ Suspected DPRK/Lazarus Group involvement (pattern-based attribution)
▫️Criticism toward Circle for delayed USDC freeze (~6h)
▫️Contagion impact: 10+ Solana protocols affected
▫️ $DRIFT token dropped ~30–37% at lows

📍 Current status:
 ▫️Investigation ongoing
 ▫️ No confirmed fund recovery yet
 ▫️Solana core infra unaffected — but DeFi trust takes a hit.

🧩 This wasn’t a smart contract bug — it was a governance + key management failure.
Durable nonces + compromised admin flow = catastrophic risk.

⚠️Stay cautious. Monitor trusted sources like Arkham, PeckShield, Elliptic, and @DriftProtocol for updates.

Everything about @DriftProtocol Exploit 

And 8 other protocols that got effected (TL;DR) 🧵👇

$270M drained

That’s 50% of their entire TVL gone

The attacker didn’t panic

Looking like they had pre-funded wallets ready a week before

Hacker wallet:

HkGz4KmoZ7Zmk7HN6ndJ31UJ1qZ2qgwQxgVqQwovpZES

This wallet was funded 8 days ago via near intents, but was inactive and suddenly received huge amounts from drift vault

Then today they pulled the trigger

In under an hour:

$155M in JLP
$60M in USDC
$11M in CBBTC
$4.7M in WETH
dozens of other assets

They swapped everything, bridged off Solana, and bought $82M+ in ETH

Deposited SOL directly into Binance and HyperLiquid

Token $DRIFT down over 35%
And it’s not over

Funds are still moving. Right now, and hacker is using @phantom, which made them already $10k in fees, lol

The exploiter currently holds $269.7M spread across wallets:

$141M in SOL
$82M in ETH
$18M in BTC

Hacker was still swapping millions of SOL to USDC and bridging out almost 3 hours after the hack

Didn’t seem like a rushed attack

It was planned, coordinated, and executed on April Fool’s Day on purpose

One of the team members left from protocol one month before it has happen 

People think maybe there is connection

But, a lot of other protocols got effected by Drift hack...

Here is full list:

@reflectmoney - USDC+ is farming yield on Drift; mint and redemption of USDC+ has been frozen; funds are insured  

@DeFiCarrot - CRT yield bearing token, team confirmed that Carrot has been impacted by the recent exploit on the Drift protocol

@project0 - protocol paused; no new borrows can be made agains drift positions 

@piggybank_fi - $106.4k/$4M of funds were deployed to Drift (~2.6%)  

@ranger_finance - rUSD has exposure to Drift, but no statement from the team so far 

- uses Drift for yield and credt; user funds withput open loans are moved away for security reasons 

@TradeNeutral - Classic deposits may be affected according to the team 

@xplaceapp- uses Drift (besides Kamino) for yield on Savings, no statement from the team so far  

@elementaldefi - uses Drift for USDC Vaults, no statement from the team so far 

There no information how exactly hack happend

Stay safe, protect your funds, and be strong to everyone who lost money 🙏

(Some of the information I got from @_0xaryan and @derparsel thx to them!)

🚨JUST IN: Onchain investigator @zachxbt has released the “Circle $USDC files,” alleging $420M+ in compliance failures since 2022, including multiple cases where hacked or illicit funds were not frozen while other issuers acted faster.

The report also includes the recent Drift exploit, where 232M+ USDC was bridged from Solana to Ethereum over six hours without being frozen.

Hack was a Private Key exploit 

​Here is how the hacker did it: 🧵👇

​Hacker gained access to Admin signer privileges

​Created a new collateral market for a worthless token (CVT)

​Jacked the Drift USDC limit from $25M to $500M

​Used CVT to borrow and drain assets from the protocol

Two Drift multisig keys appear to have been compromised

The ongoing Drift exploit has now reached $285M

Got info from @SolanaFloor, follow them for more detailed information

Critical information of parties related to the exploit have been identified. Drift is now sending an on-chain message from 0x0934faC45f2883dd5906d09aCfFdb5D18aAdC105 to the ETH Wallets that holds the stolen funds.

Wallet 1: 0xAa843eD65C1f061F111B5289169731351c5e57C1 (Timestamp of message: Apr-03-2026 05:17:23 AM +UTC)

Wallet 2: 0xD3FEEd5DA83D8e8c449d6CB96ff1eb06ED1cF6C7 (Apr-03-2026 05:20:59 AM +UTC)

Wallet 3: 0xbDdAE987FEe930910fCC5aa403D5688fB440561B (Apr-03-2026 05:23:11 AM +UTC)

Wallet 4: 0x0FE3b6908318B1F630daa5B31B49a15fC5F6B674 (Apr-03-2026 05:25:11 AM +UTC)

We are ready to speak.

Please reach out via Blockscan chat.

To the community, Drift will share further updates as soon as third-party attributions are completed.

🚨 @DriftProtocol was hit by a major exploit on April 1, with ~$270–285M drained from vaults within ~1 hour via suspicious transactions.

Initial findings suggest the attacker leveraged pre-signed durable nonce transactions combined with possible social engineering (rather than a typical smart contract bug), moving funds across chains to Ethereum to obfuscate flows.

Drift has paused deposits/withdrawals, confirmed an active attack, and is working with security partners and exchanges to contain damage while investigations continue — users are advised to revoke approvals and stay alert as the situation develops.

🚨JUST IN:  Following the Drift exploit, @DeFiCarrot says $CRT holders are facing an estimated 50% loss if no funds are recovered, and has asked users to unwind and withdraw Boost positions and redeem Turbo tokens as it works to open $CRT redemptions at adjusted NAV. https://t.co/w8lyE1Xgyk

Something doesn’t add up with Circle

A week go, they froze USDC across 16 unrelated wallets for a sealed civil case with no clarity

But during the ~$280M Drift exploit

> $230M+ USDC bridged out
> 100+ transactions
> 6 hours to respond

And most importantly minted $500M USDC at the same time of hack

Same issuer, but NO actions taken to help in any way

Hard not to question how these decisions get made?

Here is everything I'm doing during bear market ✍️

• Yield:
- conservative USDC lending (5%-7%)

- @orogoldapp points

- using Seeker/staking $SKR

• Accumulation:
- mostly adding SOL and BTC on dips

What are you doing in this market? 🤔

80085-sol, three times. $35, $42, $149. same token, same strat, just kept farming it.

narrative was great last night and the token survived, so i kept going. volume was sitting at 200k-350k per 5 minutes, TVL was low, pool was on 5% base fee. that combination is exactly what you want for spot tight. 

pool link in the comments if you want to farm this too.

the strat is spot -10%, single side sol. the exit rule is strict: when price touches about half of my position and starts going out of range.

i withdraw and reposition if it's still pumping. no waiting, no hoping it comes back in range. out, reassess, redeploy.

that's why the third position held for 6 minutes and made $149. the first two were quick cycles, the third one caught a bigger move and i let it run until the same exit rule triggered.

but this strat needs real self-control. the moment you start thinking "let me hold a bit more" or "it'll come back in range" you've already broken the logic.

the whole edge is in executing the exit exactly when the condition hits, not when it feels right.

if you don't trust yourself to exit on the rule, this strat won't work for you. it's that simple.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

pertanyaan yang paling sering masuk: "kalau gua install Meridian dan langsung running, langsung profit?"

jawaban singkatnya: ngga.

framework-nya bisa gua build. tapi edge dari Meridian bukan dari framework-nya. edge-nya adalah dari lo sendiri.

tujuan agent ini adalah mindah otak dan flow lo dalam DLMM ke dalam agent itu. strategi, preferensi, cara milih token, cara nentuin range, cara lo exit. semua itu yang bikin agent kalian jadi kalian

pastel hasilin $102 dalam sehari. prune $153 dengan 76% win rate. mereka bukan pemula yang baru install terus untung. 

mereka udah jago DLMM di Meteora, dan yang mereka lakuin adalah transfer ilmu mereka ke dalam agent ini. dari situ agent-nya jalan sendiri dengan cara pikir yang sama.

gua tanya ke orang-orang yang dapet $50 ke atas per hari dari agent ini. rata-rata mereka main dengan modal 2 SOL per posisi, maksimal 3 posisi jalan bersamaan.

kalau lo belum ngerti DLMM sama sekali, pelajarin dulu sebelum nyoba Meridian. bukan karena agent-nya susah, tapi karena lo ga bisa transfer ilmu yang lo belum punya.

banyak yang nanya gimana cara mulai biar bisa ngerti agent kalian lagi ngapain di Meteora.

jawabannya simpel: kalian harus ngerti DLMM dulu sebelum nyoba jalanin agent. pastel yang gua quote tweet itu udah 2 tahun LP di Meteora. makanya dia bisa konsisten profit dari Meridian karena dia udah tau gimana "nyetir" agent-nya dengan bener.

Tiago explains the precise strategy the French community uses

“The precise strategy is really looking for the volume. You look for the volume. I mean, they use a tool, I think it's called, Fabriq. And then they enter, but they don't do 90%, so they go for spot. They open approximately at the top. But of course, you know, always opening a bit lower is always safer. So they go spot sol only and they do a full range. So when they mean full range is 70 bins maximum and they wait and then the volume is there, it has to be a 5% or 10% pool And then they wait for five minutes maximum and they exit with I don't know when are 4 or 5% in profit.”

“It's always like I said they’re surgeons they always going to the bubble map. The holder count looking for the narrative. For example if it's a KOL led coin, like it's a red flag, don't go for it. ”

"You have to you have to do it to get good at it. It's not simply looking at, you know, videos or stuff you really have to try. So that's also a key concept that I think is important"

“The crime of the week this week is going to be the 7 wonders. So, I'm sure you guys had seen this. It kind of fell into. If you don't, it's about seven dogs that escaped like a dog meat factory or something like that. But it turned out the whole story was like a lie.”

"And I'm also going to show you guys, I'm sure some people in the chat played this one, PIXEL. I didn't play this, thankfully.
